_source: Ownership contract for the DTA instrument sections, encoding TECH_SPEC.md §6.2 (the node graph 'Owns (DTA sections)' column). This is the machine-checkable specialist write-scope map (TECH_SPEC §9.3): each full-assessment section maps to exactly one owner, asserted at build time (CLAUDE.md §8, TECH_SPEC §6.2). Assembly rejects any out-of-scope key.

_decisions.8.5: TECH_SPEC §6.2 was SILENT on the owner of §8.5 'Offer appropriate explanations' — it lists ethics as owning 8.1, 8.2, 8.4 but omits 8.5, and no other node claims it. Per CLAUDE.md §2 (document silence is a decision to make and record, not a blocker) and §8 (assert every section maps to exactly one specialist owner or the map has a silent hole), §8.5 is assigned to the Ethics & Fairness specialist: it sits in section 8 'Transparency and explainability', the cluster ethics already owns (8.1/8.2/8.4), and explainability is an ethics-principle question. TECH_SPEC §6.2 is updated in the same change to add 8.5 to the ethics row (fixing the losing document per CLAUDE.md §2).
_decisions.12.5: §12.5 'Internal governance body review' is never a specialist section — TECH_SPEC §6.2 emits it as a flagged human action into the gap register. Marked owner 'human_action' so the 1:1 assertion does not treat it as an unowned hole.
_decisions.12.3_12.4: Residual risk (§12.3 risk summary, §12.4 overall residual) is owned by the reviewer node, not a drafting specialist (TECH_SPEC §6.2, §11). The residual rating reuses the same deterministic engine as inherent (TECH_SPEC §10, §12.3/12.4).

threshold._note: Sections 1–4 are drafted by the two threshold generalists and finalised by the reconciler (node ids threshold.generalist_a/b, threshold.reconciler). Ratings for §3.1–3.9 are COMPUTED deterministically by the rating engine (threshold.rating_engine) from generalist/reconciler consequence+likelihood inputs — no agent asserts a rating (TECH_SPEC §5.8, §10).

appendix._note: The Solution Architect writes the Implementation Plan appendix AFTER every specialist is final (node full.architect, model role 'architect') — a separate pass from its specialist-bloom sections 6.3–6.6, 6.8 (TECH_SPEC §6.2 note on the dual role).

kb_specialists._note: Corpus folder ↔ specialist mapping (corpus/<specialist>/ → kb/<specialist>.sqlite, TECH_SPEC §8.3). 1:1 with the six drafting specialists.
